narration: Your salary sounds great on paper. But once you count what your job actually costs you in time and money, your real wage might shock you.
visual: Dark background. Pay stub SVG with $55,000 gross pay, showing a question mark where taxes and deductions should be. Red question mark springs in.

narration: First, taxes hit. A fifty-five thousand dollar salary becomes about forty-two thousand nine hundred after your effective rate — and that's just the beginning.
visual: Light background. Horizontal bar labeled $55,000 animates in full width. Red chunk cuts from the right showing 22% tax. $42,900 springs in below.

narration: Twenty-seven minutes each way. That's two hundred twenty-five hours of unpaid time every year, and nobody counts it in your salary.
visual: Dark background. Animated clock with spinning hands. Car SVG drives back and forth on a road. Counter climbs to 225 hours.

narration: Then count what work costs you directly — clothes, lunches, commute gas, and stress spending. Studies show it runs about five thousand a year.
visual: Light background. Work shirt, coffee cup, gas pump, and wine glass SVGs spring in one by one. Dollar counter climbs to $5,000.

narration: Real math: thirty-seven thousand nine hundred dollars divided by twenty-two twenty-five actual hours. Your true hourly wage is seventeen dollars. Wild, right?
visual: Dark background. Three sequential calculation cards: (1) $42,900 minus $5,000 equals $37,900, (2) 2,000 plus 225 equals 2,225 hrs, (3) $37,900 divided by 2,225 equals $17/hr springs in big.

narration: Now reprice everything in real hours. That forty-dollar dinner is two-point-three real hours of work. Follow for money math that changes everything.
visual: Light background. Price tag SVG ($40) with arrow to 2.3 HRS. Then three rows: $200 shoes equals 11.8 hrs, $500 phone equals 29.4 hrs, $30K car equals 1,765 hrs. Follow CTA.
